pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 14 has been expanded into a full tour-ordeal chapter: Doug's bad prepared material is now audible in all three cities, the audience reactions are concrete, and the chapter lands the required state shift while clearing the minimum length.

Single finding addressed: roughened Doug's Detroit breakthrough riff (lines 341-365) so the monologue sounds discovered in real time rather than workshop-polished. Added one self-correction ('green/teal'), one false start ('I stopped at — I think four'), one over-specific dead-end detail ('part of a Denny's sign'), and one thinking-out-loud moment ('I don't know what that smells like, but it's in there'). Removed four detachable aphoristic constructions and replaced them with flatter, more literal phrasing consistent with Doug's style-bible register (flat, literal, only accidentally incisive). No other changes to the chapter.
Revised chapter_14's closing call to preserve the must-land failure beats while compressing repeated explanation and sharpening the taping pressure around material Doug cannot deliberately reproduce.
